places.name: Blue Moon Bakery
places.address: 120 Big Pine Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: A Town Center staple - good pastries, solid coffee, and a relaxed pace. easy to reach from Town Center, and a natural first stop before a hike or a day on the mountain.

places.name: Cafe 191
places.address: 47995 Gallatin Rd Suite 101, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Reliable breakfast-and-brunch stop right on 191 with big plates, strong coffee, and an easy choice when you are heading in or out of Big Sky.

places.name: Cafe 191
places.address: 47995 Gallatin Rd Suite 101, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Reliable roadside lunch stop on 191 with burgers, sandwiches, and an easy sit-down option when you are heading in or out of Big Sky. Lunch service starts at 11:00 AM.
places.hoursText: Daily 6:00 AM - 3:00 PM / Lunch starts at 11:00 AM

places.name: Caliber Coffee
places.address: 80 Snowy Mountain Cir, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Small-batch local roaster with strong coffee, a quick breakfast rhythm, and an easy stop before getting out into town.

places.name: Cowboy Coffee Co.
places.address: 25 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: The locals' coffee shop. Nothing fancy, consistently good espresso, and a line out the door on busy mornings. Worth the wait.

places.name: The Corral
places.address: 42895 Gallatin Rd, Big Sky, MT 59730
places.summary: Classic Big Sky breakfast spot - big plates, local feel, and the kind of place where staff and regulars know each other. Good choice when guests want to sit down and take their time.

places.name: Unravel Coffee + Bar
places.address: 88 Big Pine Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Inside Gravity Haus - coffee service runs through the day, but breakfast service is a morning window before the space shifts toward tacos and bar service later on.
places.hoursText: Coffee daily 7:00 AM - 8:00 PM / Breakfast 7:00 - 11:00 AM

places.name: Wildflower Market & Cafe
places.address: 995 Settlement Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: An elegant cafe and marketplace at Montage with French-flour pastries, specialty coffee, and artisan grab-and-go items - a high-quality breakfast for guests wanting something a touch more refined.

places.name: Beartooth Pub & Rec
places.address: 995 Settlement Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Inside Montage - flatbreads from a hearth oven, solid pub food, bowling lanes, darts, and arcade games. Works well for groups or families who want more than just a meal.

places.name: By Word of Mouth
places.address: 77 Aspen Leaf Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Town Center bistro with weekday lunch and dinner service. easy Town Center option and still one of the better casual local picks in the core.
places.hoursText: Mon - Fri 11:30 AM - 9:00 PM (closed Sat - Sun)

places.name: Chet's Bar & Grill
places.address: 44 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Basic mountain bar food done well - burger, cold beer, and a seat near the resort. Practical pick when guests are already up at Mountain Village and don't want to drive down.
places.hoursText: Daily, Lunch 11:00 AM - 2:30 PM / Dinner 5:00 - 10:00 PM

places.name: Scissorbills Saloon
places.address: 39 Black Eagle Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Small Montana saloon at Mountain Village - nachos, burgers, and the best apres-ski energy in the area. Live music most winter nights. Nothing precious about it.

places.name: Terrain Taco
places.address: 88 Big Pine Dr, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Tacos and margaritas inside Gravity Haus with a patio when the weather's right. Locally sourced proteins, creative fillings - a solid lunch especially after a morning on the mountain.

places.name: Tips Up
places.address: 76 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Big Sky's social hub - cold beers, burgers, live music, pool tables, and cornhole outside. The right call for casual groups where afternoon drinks tend to turn into an evening.
places.hoursText: Sun food 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM / Mon - Wed 3:00 - 10:00 PM / Thu - Sat 3:00 - 11:00 PM

places.name: Alpenglow Bar
places.address: 995 Settlement Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: The lobby bar at Montage - floor-to-ceiling mountain views, fireplaces, live piano, craft cocktails. Even if guests aren't staying there, it's worth sending them for a drink.
places.hoursText: Daily 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM / Bar until 11:00 PM

places.name: Blend Big Sky
places.address: Town Center, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: A proper wine bar, small and quiet - French pours and Montana selections. Good for guests who want to wind down rather than go out. Not the place for a group, perfect for two.

places.name: Blue Buddha Sushi Lounge
places.address: 99 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Big Sky's sushi spot - fresh rolls, Japanese cocktails, pool tables and DJs later in the evening. A good change of pace from the steak-and-burger rotation.
places.hoursText: Tue - Sat 5:00 - 9:00 PM / Happy hour 5:00 - 6:00 PM

places.name: Cabin Bar & Grill
places.address: 33 Black Eagle Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: A polished Mountain Village dinner with slope views and a quieter room than the busier apres spots nearby. Best when guests want dinner to feel a little more special.

places.name: Copper Whiskey Bar & Grill
places.address: 145 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Montana steaks, good burgers, and a serious whiskey selection by the fire. Right pick when guests want something heartier and a bit more classic.

places.name: Cortina
places.address: 995 Settlement Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Handmade pasta and a polished room at the Mountain Village. One of the better Italian dinners in the area - right for guests who want something refined without it being a full production.

places.name: Everett's 8800
places.address: 444 Andesite Ridge Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Guests ride the chairlift up to dinner at 8,800 feet. Alpine tasting menu, seasonal, spectacular views. One of the more unique restaurant experiences in the state - winter only.
places.hoursText: Lunch daily 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM / Dinner Wed - Sat 5:00 - 9:00 PM (winter)

places.name: Horn & Cantle
places.address: 750 Lone Mountain Ranch Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Lone Mountain Ranch dining room - the place when someone asks for the most Big Sky dinner experience possible. Great for birthdays, anniversaries, or guests who want to feel like they're really here.
places.hoursText: Daily, Dinner 5:00 - 9:00 PM / Saloon open from noon

places.name: M by The Alinea Group
places.address: 48 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Winter-only pop-up from the Alinea Group out of Chicago. Tasting menu and a la carte. The kind of dinner guests talk about for years - book well in advance.

places.name: Montana Jack
places.address: 52 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Laid-back Town Center spot good for families and groups who want a straightforward dinner without the formality. Burgers, bowls, easy patio - never a complicated recommendation.

places.name: Tres Toros Tacos & Tequila
places.address: 145 Town Center Ave, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Birria tacos, huckleberry margaritas, and a serious tequila list. Lively and casual - one of the more fun dinners in Town Center, especially for groups.
places.hoursText: Mon - Wed & Fri - Sun 3:00 - 10:00 PM (closed Thu)

places.name: Wild Caddis at Rainbow Ranch
places.address: 42950 Gallatin Rd, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
places.summary: Dinner along the Gallatin River at Rainbow Ranch - log-and-stone dining room, ranch-sourced menu. Worth the drive down the canyon for a special night out.

places.name: Adventure Mountain
places.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: The activity hub at Mountain Village - zip lines, mini golf, a climbing wall, and bungee all in one spot. Easy to fill a half day, especially with kids. No need to plan ahead.

places.name: Beehive Basin
places.address: 39620 Moose Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: One of the best hikes in the area, full stop. Nine and a half miles round trip with real elevation, ending in an alpine basin surrounded by peaks. Plan for a full day and tell guests to bring layers.

places.name: Big Sky Resort Golf Course
places.address: 2100 Black Otter Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Arnold Palmer-designed, 18 holes at altitude with serious elevation changes and views of the Spanish Peaks. Plays differently than most courses guests are used to - a good challenge.
places.hoursText: Summer seasonal / Call for tee times: (406) 995-5780

places.name: Big Sky Resort Mountain Biking
places.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Over 40 miles of lift-served trail at the resort, from easy flow runs to technical terrain. Rentals on site. One of the better lift-served bike parks in the West - worth recommending to any guest who rides.

places.name: Big Sky Resort Scenic Lift Rides
places.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: The gondola runs in summer for guests who want to get up on the mountain without the hike. Great views of the Spanish Peaks from the top - easy to recommend for any fitness level.

places.name: Big Sky Resort Ziplines
places.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Long lines through the trees in Huntley Hollow - the guided nature tour is good for families and the straight zipline run is fast. One of the better ways to see the mountain without hiking.

places.name: Canyon Adventures Horseback Riding
places.address: 47200 Gallatin Rd, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
places.summary: One- to three-hour guided rides into the Gallatin National Forest. Reliable operator, good for first-timers and families. Guests mention this one consistently when they come back.

places.name: Gallatin River Guides
places.address: 47430 Gallatin Rd, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
places.summary: The oldest fly shop in Big Sky and still the best call for fishing. They guide the Gallatin and run trips into Yellowstone. Half days work well for guests who haven't fished before.

places.name: Lone Mountain Ranch Sleigh Ride Dinner
places.address: 750 Lone Mountain Ranch Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Sleigh ride out to the North Fork Cabin for a prime rib dinner - about 20 minutes each way through the trees. Books up quickly in peak season. Winter only, and guests remember it.

places.name: Montana Whitewater
places.address: 63960 Gallatin Rd, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
places.summary: The Gallatin River runs right through the canyon and Montana Whitewater puts guests on it. The half-day family run is the most popular; the Class IV stretch is legitimate whitewater for guests who want that.
places.hoursText: Trips daily 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Memorial Day - Labor Day)

places.name: Ousel Falls
places.address: Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: about ten minutes away and genuinely worth the stop. Easy 1.5-mile round trip through the trees to a 30-foot waterfall. Gets crowded midday - earlier or later in the afternoon is much better.

places.name: Solace Spa & Recovery Lounge
places.address: 44 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Full-service spa at the resort - massage, facials, and recovery equipment. Couple's suite available. Good recommendation after a big ski or hiking day. Book ahead in peak weeks.

places.name: Spa Montage
places.address: 995 Settlement Trail, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: The biggest spa in the area - 12 treatment rooms, heated pools, steam, and an indoor pool. guests can book with a reservation. Right call for guests who take their spa time seriously.
places.hoursText: Spa 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM / Treatments 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM

places.name: Warren Miller Performing Arts Center
places.address: 45465 Gallatin Rd, Big Sky, MT 59730
places.summary: A genuinely good venue for live music, theater, and local performances. Worth checking what's on during the guest's stay - the bigger shows sell out.
places.hoursText: Event dependent - check warrenmillerpac.org for schedule

places.name: Yellowstone National Park Day Tour
places.address: Pickup details provided after booking
places.summary: About 45 miles south and worth a full day. Multiple operators run guided tours covering wildlife, geysers, and hot springs. Having a guide makes a real difference in what guests actually see.

places.name: Adventure Mountain
places.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: The activity plaza at Mountain Village - zip lines, mini golf, climbing wall, and bungee trampoline all in one spot. No planning needed; easy to fill a half day.

places.name: Big Sky Community Park
places.address: 25 Seeway Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Open green space with a playground and room to run. Simple and free - the right call when kids need to burn energy without an agenda.

places.name: Big Sky Resort Ziplines
places.address: 50 Big Sky Resort Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Long lines through the trees at the resort - fast, safe, and great for kids. The guided nature tour version works well for younger ones who want a bit more to it than just the zip.

places.name: Canyon Adventures Horseback Riding
places.address: 47200 Gallatin Rd, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
places.summary: Walking-pace trail rides through the National Forest - appropriate for most ages and no experience needed. A reliable pick for families; kids consistently enjoy it.

places.name: Montana Whitewater
places.address: 63960 Gallatin Rd, Gallatin Gateway, MT 59730
places.summary: Class II-III runs on the Gallatin - splashy and fun, not scary. All gear provided, and kids genuinely love it. One of the better family half-days in Big Sky.

places.name: Ousel Falls
places.address: Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Short, easy trail with a waterfall payoff - kids love it. about ten minutes away, 1.5 miles round trip. Good for any age that can walk.

places.name: Yellowstone National Park Day Tour
places.address: Pickup details provided after booking
places.summary: 45 miles south and a full day well spent. Geysers, bison, hot springs - having a guide matters because they know where to be and when. Great for kids of any age.

places.name: The Waypoint
places.address: 50 Ousel Falls Rd, Big Sky, MT 59716
places.summary: Cinema, bar, and dinner stop in Town Center with a stronger Sunday night value play if you want to pair dinner with a movie.

deals.name: By Word of Mouth
deals.description: Happy hour combo: MAP Party Lager, pineapple shot, and the BYWOM burger for $19.

deals.name: The Waypoint
deals.description: Dinner and a Movie Sundays: one free movie ticket and one small concession drink per entree ordered.

local.title: Big Sky Connect - Local Rides
local.description: On-demand, fare-free local transit within the Big Sky service area. Request rides at the time of need - no advance scheduling. Does not serve the airport.
bullets: Winter (Nov 24 - Apr 26): Town Center & Meadow Village 6am-11pm / Mountain Village 7:30am-9:30pm

airport.title: Airport Transportation
airport.description: Big Sky Connect does not serve Bozeman Yellowstone International Airport (BZN). Yellowstone Limousine offers private transfers for the 50-mile trip.
bullets: BZN is approximately 50 miles from Big Sky
Yellowstone Limousine - private car service to/from BZN
Book in advance - demand is high during peak season

notes: Rewrote obvious hotel-specific copy to destination-neutral language.
Removed front-desk fallback from transport actions.
